name: Barbara's Milk Chocolate Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 7 ounces milk chocolate (Heatter uses Hershey's Symphony bar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the almonds in a shallow pan in a preheated 350°F oven for 12 to 15 minutes, shaking the pan once or twice. Remove and set aside to cool.
2. Cut or break the chocolate into small pieces and place it in the bowl of a food processor fitted with the metal chopping blade. Let stand.
3. Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, cocoa powder, and sugar. Add about 1 cup of the flour mixture to the chocolate. Process for about 30 seconds, or until the chocolate is fine and powdery.
4. In a large bowl, mix together the processed ingredients with the remaining sifted dry ingredients. Stir in the almonds.
5. Beat the eggs with vanilla extract just to mix. With a large rubber or wooden spatula, stir the egg mixture into the dry ingredients. You may think there is too much liquid; it's okay.
6. Place two 20-inch lengths of plastic wrap on the work surface.
7. To shape two loaves, spoon a strip of the dough down the middle of each piece of plastic wrap. Each strip should be 13 to 14 inches long. Flatten the tops. Lift the two long sides of the plastic wrap and press on the plastic wrap to smooth the dough into an even strip, 2 1/2 to 3 inches wide and 3/4 inch thick (no thicker), with squared ends.
8. Transfer to a cookie sheet and place in the freezer for about 2 hours (or longer) until firm enough to retain the log shape when the plastic wrap is removed.
9. Adjust two racks to divide the oven into thirds and preheat to 300°F. Line two large cookie sheets with baking parchment or aluminum foil, shiny-side up.
10. Open the long sides of the plastic wrap on one strip of dough and turn the dough upside down on the lined sheet, placing it diagonally on the sheet. Peel off the plastic wrap. Repeat with the second strip and the second sheet.
11. Bake the strips for 1 hour, reversing the sheets top to bottom and front to back once during baking to ensure even baking. The strips will spread out (7 to 8 inches wide) during baking.
12. After 1 hour of baking, reduce the oven temperature to 275°F and remove the sheets from the oven. Immediately, with a wide metal spatula, release one strip from the parchment or foil and place it on a board. Repeat with the second strip.
13. Use a pot holder or folded towel to hold one of the hot strips in place, and use a serrated French bread knife to cut the strip crosswise into slices 1/2 to 3/4 inch wide. Repeat with the second strip.
14. Place the slices upright on unlined cookie sheets with a little space between them.
15. Return to oven to bake at 275°F for 40 minutes, reversing the sheets top to bottom and front to back once during baking.
16. Let cool and store in an airtight container.
Per biscotto: 80 calories, 2 gm protein, 11 gm carbohydrates, 4 gm fat, 17 mg cholesterol, 1 gm saturated fat, 41 mg sodium
The dough must spend about 2 hours in the freezer before baking (it can stay longer). There are two bakings, which will total about 1 hour and 40 minutes.
